content: agentfront issue #35 spec CONVERGED (docs/specs/2026-06-26-agentfront-ships-a-public-consumer-cli-api-a-host.md) via /think. Key DECISION (user, 2026-06-26): the MCP surface ships as a SINGLE-DISPATCH "CLI on MCP" tool ONLY — one MCP tool taking a command-path + args, dispatching through the App registry, structured {code,message,remediation} on failure — NOT N tools. This CONFLICTS with colleague’s converged spec (colleague repo docs/specs/2026-06-25-colleague-s-agent-first-cli-is-rendered-from-an-im.md), whose honesty condition asserts CLI/MCP/learn enumerate the SAME set (= N MCP tools). RESOLUTION: colleague MUST revise that set-equality condition to compare the single tool’s command catalog (colleague-side follow-up; coordinate via an agentculture/colleague issue). Other agentfront #35 decisions: grow App.cli() to dispatch registered tools (not a new build_cli); nested groups via explicit group= on registration; app.add_command(name,handler) for host-owned launcher verbs (session/tui); promote AND RENAME the internal structured-error type to a PUBLIC AgentfrontError {code,message,remediation} in an argparse-free module (agentfront.errors) — completing the deferred PR #22 rename, removing the retired-prefix class name entirely (flagged by Qodo on PR #36); reuse the internal agentfront/cli + explain + overview machinery by parameterizing prog/version/issues_url out of _brand; pure-stdlib (mcp stays the [mcp] extra). explain/overview are NOT registry-derived today (hand-authored catalog + filesystem walk) — the consumer API must add a registry-derivation path.

Operational learning (agentfront issue #35 implementation, /assign-to-workforce with /ask-colleague as worker on a local vLLM Qwen3.6-27B): a 13-task TDD-gated plan was fully implemented this way and landed 423 passing tests (PR agentculture/agentfront#37, v0.14.0). What works: route each plan task to ask-colleague write --apply with a DETAILED brief (current-state context + exact acceptance criteria + work-test-first), land a colleague/<id> branch, then the MAIN agent (Claude) runs the FULL suite as the TDD gate before/after a --no-ff merge. NEVER trust colleague`s self-reported pass count or its mid-run `affected-tests: N failed` line (frequently transient/wrong) — always run the gate yourself. The 27B backend handled design+bulk implementation well (even the CLI-builder centerpiece passed as-is), but two failure modes recur: (1) runs OUT OF STEPS on the mechanical tail of multi-file refactors (e.g. a rename) — bump --max-steps (40 default is too low; use 50-90), and the main agent finishes the last-mile + adds the missing acceptance test; (2) TIMES OUT on big-context tasks (vLLM 300s request limit, zero output) — raise COLLEAGUE_TIMEOUT=600, and if it still fails, the main agent writes that (well-scoped) task directly. Sequential execution on a single backend NEUTRALIZES same-wave file-collision risk (each task builds on the prior merge). colleague-generated code usually needs a lint pass (unused imports, isort, over-broad pytest.raises) before CI is green. Pattern in one line: colleague drafts, main reconciles the last mile, the TDD gate is the arbiter.

agentfront issue #38 spec CONVERGED (docs/specs/2026-06-26-agentfront-closes-the-two-consumer-api-gaps-collea.md) via /think. Two consumer-API gaps colleague (first import adopter) hit migrating to 0.14.0 import-rendered CLI. Ask 1: Flag gains choices: Sequence[str]|None=None, forwarded to argparse add_argument(choices=...) in _flag_kwargs so out-of-set values reject at parse time through the existing _CliParser structured-error path. Ask 2: expose the single MCP run tool publicly. DECISION (user, 2026-06-26): run-tool accessor is a .run_tool attribute on the Server returned by app.mcp_server() (built internally from _build_run_tool which stays private), NOT a public build_run_tool function, NOT both. DECISION (user): c10 (explain surfaces choices) is IN SCOPE — add choices-aware flag rendering to explains leaf output (text + --json) via the registry-derived path; today explain prints ONLY the leaf docstring, no flags, so this needs new flag-rendering, not just an argparse passthrough. Boundary: NOT a broader Flag overhaul (no Union/Optional schema work), NOT a move to N MCP tools. Lockstep: teken wrapper + CHANGELOG + version bump required.

agentfront gotcha (verified 0.15.0): a Flag declared via @app.tool(flags=(...)) is parsed AND validated by argparse, but only reaches the function if the function signature has a matching param — _make_dispatcher in cli_surface.py builds kwargs by iterating the FUNCTION sig and pulling matching namespace attrs, so flags= flags whose names arent function params (like the e2e export verbs --verbose/--format) are parsed/validated then dropped. Implication: a choices flag (e.g. --algo) rejects an out-of-set value at PARSE time regardless of whether the func consumes it — so issue #38 Ask1 tests use a flags=(Flag(--algo, choices=...)) on a func WITHOUT an algo param, avoiding the double-declaration conflict (declaring both a param `algo` with a default AND a flags= --algo would make _derive_args_from_sig and apply_flags both add --algo → argparse conflicting-option error).

agentfront gotcha: the version-bump skill (.claude/skills/version-bump/scripts/bump.py) reads changelog entries from stdin JSON with LOWERCASE section keys — {"added":[...], "changed":[...], "fixed":[...]}. Capitalized keys (Added/Changed/Fixed) parse without error but silently produce an EMPTY changelog stub (format_changelog_section only looks for added/changed/fixed). If the [X.Y.Z] section comes out empty after a bump, the keys were wrong — fix by editing CHANGELOG.md directly (do NOT re-run bump, that double-bumps the version + teken wrapper).

agentfront convention (learned from Qodo review on PR #39): when adding a field to a documented PUBLIC frozen dataclass (Flag, ToolEntry, DocEntry, HostCommand in agentfront/_registry.py), make it KEYWORD-ONLY via dataclasses.field(default=None, kw_only=True). Inserting a field in the middle (or even appending without kw_only) shifts the generated __init__ positional parameter order, silently mis-mapping a consumers positional Flag(names,type,action,nargs,dest,...) construction into the wrong fields with no error. kw_only keeps every existing fields positional slot intact while still allowing the new field=value. Guard with a test asserting the Nth positional still binds to the original field and that the new field raises TypeError when passed positionally.

Gotcha (verified 2026-06-27, agentfront #40 via ask-colleague write --apply): colleagues post-run affected-tests check prints a FALSE "N passed, 1 failed" line (it caps at ~20 of the changed-impact test files and hits an ordering/collection artifact). It does NOT mean the real suite fails — always re-run the actual suite yourself (uv run pytest -n auto -q) before trusting it. In the #40 run colleague reported 228 passed, 1 failed but the real suite was 459 passed both serial and parallel. Also: colleague write --apply runs uv run, which rewrites uv.lock (and the committed lock may be stale, e.g. mains self-version lagged at 0.14.0 while pyproject was 0.15.0) — revert the stray uv.lock and run uv lock after the version bump so the lockfile self-version matches pyproject.

agex/devex pr reply JSONL schema (from devex/commands/pr/scripts/reply.py, not in the cicd skill docs): each stdin line is one JSON object {"body": str (required), "in_reply_to": <numeric review-comment id> (optional, threads the reply under an inline comment), "thread_id": <GraphQL review-thread node_id e.g. PRRT_...> (optional, triggers pr_resolve_thread)}. Omit in_reply_to for a top-level PR comment. agex auto-signs each body once with "- <nick> (Claude)" (skips if already signed) — do NOT sign manually. Get comment ids via: gh api repos/<org>/<repo>/pulls/<pr>/comments --jq .[].id ; thread node_ids appear in cicd status under Unresolved threads. Run: bash .claude/skills/cicd/scripts/workflow.sh reply <PR> < replies.jsonl

agentfront TAUI spec CONVERGED via /think (docs/specs/2026-06-27-agentfront-ships-taui-as-its-fourth-generated-surf.md). TAUI = the FOURTH agent-facing surface after CLI/MCP/HTTP: one state -> JSON mirror (agent baseline, stable ids+selectors+available_actions), ANSI/TUI (human terminal), markdown (readable); agent and human fold through the SAME reducer (selector-dispatched action == keystroke). 4 USER DECISIONS (2026-06-27): (1) DELIVERY = IMPORT NOT SCAFFOLD — app.taui()/app.taui_mirror()/app.taui_driver() render at runtime from the App config, like app.cli()/app.mcp_server(); NOT scaffolded source the consumer owns (per the runtime pivot). (2) RENDERING DEP = STDLIB-ONLY hand-rolled ANSI, zero third-party (colleague precedent + outside-org dep policy); any textual/rich path is a future opt-in [taui] extra, never a base dep. (3) STATE PROVENANCE = HYBRID — agentfront auto-derives a BASELINE cockpit from the command registry (commands->panels/actions/selectors), author EXTENDS with popups/zones/custom panels/work feeds. (4) V1 SCOPE = pure deterministic CORE (state+reduce+render tiers+JSON mirror+selector dispatch+replay/snapshot) + a THIN reference TTY driver; defer production live loop (colleague found live-wiring most error-prone: inert popup buttons/quit-traps). COLLEAGUE TIE-IN = LIFT & GENERALIZE colleague's proven tui/ (state,events,reducer,taui serialize,selectors,render tiers,diagnose,tui_sim) into agentfront; colleague then imports agentfront's TAUI and deletes its copy. Honesty/test contract: one to_dict() is sole source; available_actions derived from tree (every selector resolves); agent-path==human-path identical resulting state; cross-render diagnose invariant clean; colleague's lifted tests pass unchanged; no new base dep. devague gotcha hit + worked around: blocking hard questions q1-q3 unclearable via CLI, set resolved:true in .devague/frames/<slug>.json; exporter drops resolved flag so the spec's Hard-questions section was hand-patched.

agentfront TAUI implementation seam (synthesized from a colleague explore + a Sonnet Explore subagent, 2026-06-27). SEAM: all agent-facing surfaces are LAZY FACTORY METHODS on App (app.cli()->cli_surface.make_cli, app.mcp_server()->mcp_surface.make_mcp_server, app.http_app()->http_surface.make_http_app), each enumerating from app.list_tools()/app.list_docs() over the single Registry (agentfront/_registry.py). TAUI follows the SAME pattern: add agentfront/taui_surface.py exporting make_taui(app)/make_taui_mirror(app)/make_taui_driver(app) + app.taui()/taui_mirror()/taui_driver() accessors in app.py. Stdlib-only -> NO [taui] extra (only [mcp] is optional today). CRITICAL CORRECTION (Sonnet caught colleague's error): consumer explain/overview (cli_surface.py _explain_handler/_overview_handler) are ALREADY fully registry-derived; only agentfront-the-tool's OWN cli (explain/catalog.py ENTRIES dict) is hand-authored, so the 'catalog drift' risk does NOT apply to the consumer TAUI path. CROSS-RENDER INVARIANT GATE (the enforcement mechanism for our honesty condition): agentfront/serve.py surfaces_agree()/surface_inventory() + agentfront/_dogfood.py compare registry==cli==mcp==http tools/docs in CI; TAUI must extend Surfaces dataclass/build_surfaces()/surface_inventory()/surfaces_agree() so 'cross-render invariant runs clean' is enforced by the existing dogfood gate. REGISTRY->TAUI MAPPING: ToolEntry.group->panels (group[0]=panel id), name->action id/selector ('.'.join(group+(name,)) e.g. 'feedback.record'), description->label, doc->detail pane, flags+input_schema->action params, aliases->alt selectors; DocEntry->docs panel; App.name/version/description->header. NOTE: HostCommand (app.add_command, host CLI verbs) is SEPARATE from ToolEntry (app.tool) — TAUI available_actions must merge app.list_tools() + app.list_commands(). AUTHOR-EXTENSIBLE OVERLAY (registry canNOT express, author adds as extra nodes in the same to_dict() tree): zones, popups/dialogs, work feed/event log, focus model, key bindings, conditional availability, input routing, status/health indicators, custom panels. derive_input_schema only maps base types (Optional/Union/generics fall back to 'string') -> complex param widgets are a v1 limitation. Tests: tests/unit/test_<module>.py in-process via run_cli(app,argv); tests/integration/ for cross-surface invariants/dogfood. Line numbers omitted (drift) — grep the named symbols.

agentfront TAUI build PLAN converged + exported via /spec-to-plan (docs/plans/2026-06-27-agentfront-ships-taui-as-its-fourth-generated-surf.md), from the converged frame agentfront-ships-taui-as-its-fourth-generated-surf. 15 tasks, 6 file-disjoint waves, all 13 coverage targets covered. Package = agentfront/taui/. Waves: W1 t1 state.py + t2 events.py; W2 t3 derive.py(registry baseline, merges list_tools()+list_commands()) + t4 selectors.py + t5 reducer.py(single fold for agent SelectorAction & human KeyPress) + t6 render/ansi.py + t7 render/markdown.py + t13 dep-guard test(stdlib-only/boundary); W3 t8 mirror.py(available_actions derived from tree, taui_version); W4 t9 diagnose.py(cross-render invariant) + t10 driver.py(thin ref TTY); W5 t11 app.py accessors app.taui()/taui_mirror()/taui_driver(); W6 t12 serve.py+dogfood gate(surfaces_agree incl TAUI) + t14 e2e flow(agent mirror+selector == human keystrokes, identical state) + t15 colleague-parity(colleague's TAUI tests pass unchanged). Risks: r1 follow_up = colleague-side migration (import agentfront.taui, delete its tui/ — file agentculture/colleague issue); r2 unknown_nonblocking = complex param widgets deferred (derive_input_schema base-types only). USER decided 2026-06-27: implement via /assign-to-workforce with ask-colleague as worker (same pattern as issue #35). devague plan confirm takes ONE task id at a time (not batch, unlike frame confirm).

agentfront TAUI (fourth surface) SHIPPED as PR #42 (https://github.com/agentculture/agentfront/pull/42), v0.17.0, branch feat/taui-surface. Built end-to-end via the full pipeline in ONE session: /think (converged spec) -> /spec-to-plan (15-task plan) -> /assign-to-workforce. Execution: ask-colleague (local vLLM Qwen3.6-27B) drafted ~14 module/test tasks via `write --apply` (one colleague/<id> branch each), ONE task (t15 colleague-parity, needs ../colleague read access) done by a Sonnet subagent in an isolated worktree; main agent (Opus) TDD-gated EVERY --no-ff merge (full suite before+after) + reconciled lint (B033 dup-set, isort). Result: 696 tests passing (+234 TAUI), dogfood gate prints 'four surfaces, and they agree' (surfaces_agree+taui_diagnose True), CI green, Qodo 0 bugs. Package agentfront/taui/: state,events,reducer(single fold agent SelectorAction==human KeyPress, navigation-parity v1),selectors,derive(registry baseline),mirror(available_actions derived from tree),diagnose(cross-render),render/{ansi,markdown},driver(thin ref). app.taui()/taui_mirror()/taui_driver() accessors. serve.surfaces_agree + _dogfood extended. Colleague review (rated 4/5) actioned: made mirror.serialize() non-throwing (diagnose is the validator), documented v1 navigation-only scope, Header.title default. OPERATIONAL LEARNINGS: ask-colleague write briefs MUST be detailed (current-state context + exact acceptance + work-test-first + 'this task is ONLY file X'); pre-create shared package __init__ files (render/) so same-wave tasks don't collide on them; lint check must NOT pipe flake8 through `tail` (masks exit code) — run flake8 directly + check $?; colleague output reliably needs an isort/black/flake8-B033 reconcile pass before the gate is green; cross-repo tasks (need a sibling repo) can't use colleague's sandboxed worktree -> route to a Sonnet subagent with isolation:worktree. FOLLOW-UPS (open): (1) colleague-side migration — colleague imports agentfront.taui + deletes its tui/; the 12 documented PARITY GAPS in tests/integration/test_taui_colleague_parity.py ARE the checklist (file an agentculture/colleague issue); (2) cosmetic: ungrouped 'root' panel renders empty '## ' heading; (3) DocEntry docs-panel + tool-EXECUTION via SelectorAction.args deferred (v1 is navigation only).

TAUI test isolation: the three lazy-import tests in tests/unit/test_taui_app_accessors.py evict every agentfront.taui.* module from sys.modules (to prove app.taui()/taui_mirror()/taui_driver() trigger the import) and MUST restore them afterward. If they don't, app.taui() re-imports the submodules under a fresh identity, leaving the suite with TWO PanelItem class objects: make_baseline (bound at collection time) yields v1 instances while a later 'from agentfront.taui.state import PanelItem' yields v2, so isinstance(node, PanelItem) returns False. This only fails under full-suite ordering with xdist -n 2 (CI's worker count), NOT under -n auto locally (different test distribution) — a green local 'pytest -n auto' can still red CI. Fix: wrap eviction in an _evicted_taui_modules() context manager that snapshots and restores sys.modules. Lesson: when CI 'Tests' fails but local passes, reproduce with the SAME worker count (uv run pytest -n 2 -q) before assuming flakiness.

agentfront TAUI v0.2 work-loop cockpit uplift SHIPPED as PR #44 (https://github.com/agentculture/agentfront/pull/44), v0.18.0, branch feat/taui-uplift-43, closing issue #43 — closes ALL 12 colleague-parity gaps so colleague can import agentfront.taui and delete its colleague/tui/ (consumer tracker colleague#249). Authoritative checklist = the repo's OWN tests/integration/test_taui_colleague_parity.py: the 3 test_parity_gap_* smoke tests FLIP from gap-OPEN to gap-CLOSED, plus the 2 no-op tests (test_tick_is_a_noop, test_user_input_is_a_noop) flip to assert new behavior. WHAT LANDED (all stdlib-only, all frozen=True, generic-not-colleague-named): state.py Background(theme/animation/frame/semantic) + ConversationLine(text,count) with .render()->'text xN' consecutive-dup collapse, 2 new TAUIState fields (background, conversation) appended after problems; events.py SkillSuggested + WorkStep; reducer folds Tick(advance background.frame), UserInput(append+collapse), SkillSuggested(open popup.skill-suggested + set background), WorkStep(step work_item.step_count + log + error popup) + replay(); selectors.resolve() now resolves zone keys ->{'kind':'zone',...}; mirror SCHEMA_VERSION 0.1->0.2; render/ansi frame glyph by background.frame%4 + both renderers render the conversation zone; diagnose.py keeps flat diagnose()->DiagnoseResult UNCHANGED and ADDS diagnose_structured()->Diagnosis 7 bug classes (STATE/RENDER/LAYOUT/FOCUS/INPUT_ROUTING/THEME/POPUP_LIFECYCLE); NEW agentfront/taui/snapshot.py = snapshot quad (.taui.json/.ansi/.events.jsonl/.md) + replay re-export + faithful() JSON<->Markdown check. 835 tests pass; dogfood 'four surfaces and they agree' + taui_diagnose True. ORCHESTRATION (worked well): Opus-as-architect wrote ONE authoritative DESIGN CONTRACT file (exact dataclass shapes + signatures + per-wave file ownership) to scratchpad, then drove Sonnet subagents (Agent tool model:sonnet) in 4 dependency-ordered waves with file-disjoint parallelism within a wave (w1 state+events; w2 reducer/selectors/mirror/renderers parallel; w3 diagnose+snapshot parallel; w4 parity flips by Opus); each subagent ran ONLY its own test files + flake8 ONLY its files (avoids concurrent-tree thrash); Opus verified full suite + reconciled black/isort/flake8 between waves; then a Sonnet REVIEW + Sonnet LIVE-TEST in parallel. REVIEW CAUGHT 2 REAL SELF-INCONSISTENCIES (fixed): (1) _reduce_dismiss left blocking=True on hide so diagnose POPUP_LIFECYCLE false-positived on a DISMISSED error popup -> fix: dismiss clears blocking=False; (2) snapshot.faithful() compared raw .text not rendered 'text xN', disagreeing with renderer+diagnose RENDER on collapsed lines -> fix: render the form in faithful(). Also: repeated failed WorkStep appended duplicate-id popup.work-error (diagnose LAYOUT flagged) -> fix: refresh ONE live error popup by id. LESSON CONFIRMED: subagent output reliably needs a black/isort/flake8 reconcile pass (E501 on long string literals black won't wrap; isort order) before the gate is green. FOLLOW-UP: when PR #44 merges and publish.yml tags a release, note the tag on issue #43 AND colleague#249 so colleague pins its floor.
